---
title: "425 Project working draft"
output:
  word_document:
    fig_height: 5
    fig_width: 8
date: "April 7"
author: "Nicole Kamarat, Ella Peterson"
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE)
options(show.signif.stars = FALSE)

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(ggResidpanel)
library(ggthemes)
library(cardx)
library(easyalluvial)
library(ggmosaic)
library(catstats2)
library(ggrepel)
library(cetcolor)
library(gtsummary)
library(readxl)
library(mosaic)
library(tidyverse)
library(vcd)
library(epitools)
library(car)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(mice)
library(ggsurvfit)
library(lubridate)
library(rifttable)
library(CoxR2)
library(MuMIn)
library(emmeans)
library(multcomp)
library(splines)
library(splineplot)
library(mi)
library(dplyr)

theme_set(theme_bw()) #Prevents need for + theme_bw() in ggplots

#read in data
wildfires <- read.csv("wildfires.csv")
#create a "size growth until containment" variable
wildfires$SizeChange <- wildfires$BH_Size - wildfires$IA_Size
wildfires$logSizeChange <- log(wildfires$SizeChange)

#remove any fires that "did not grow"
wildfire1 <- wildfires %>% filter(SizeChange!=0)
#treat growth as a factor
wildfire1$Growth <- factor(wildfire1$Growth)
wildfire1$Status <- factor(wildfire1$Status)

#look at missing data
wildmdf <- missing_data.frame(as.data.frame(wildfires))
md.pattern(wildmdf)
```

##Table 1
```{r}
table1 <- wildfire1 %>%
  dplyr::select(Fuel_type, Method, Detection, FWI) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = Fuel_type,
    missing = "no",
    statistic = all_categorical() ~ "{n}",
    label = list(
      Method ~ "Method",
      Detection ~ "Detection",
      FWI ~ "Fire Weather Index"
    )
  ) %>%
  add_overall() %>%
  bold_labels() %>%
  modify_caption("**Table 1. Summary of Fire Characteristics by Fuel Type**")

# Print Table 1
table1
```

## Kaplan Meier Curve
```{r}
ggsurvfit(survfit(Surv(SizeChange, Status) ~ 1, data = wildfires)) +
  add_confidence_interval() +
  add_censor_mark() +
  add_risktable(risktable_stats = c("n.risk", "n.event")) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(
    x = "Change in Size (Hectares)",
    y = "Survival Probability"
  )

ggsurvfit(survfit(Surv(SizeChange, Status) ~ Fuel_type, data = wildfires)) +
  add_confidence_interval() +
  add_censor_mark() +
  add_risktable(risktable_stats = c("n.risk")) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(
    x = "Change in size (Hectares)",
    y = "Survival Probability",
    color = "Fuel type"
  )
```



##CONSORT Diagram

```{r}
library(DiagrammeR)

grViz("
digraph consort_wcg {
  graph [layout = dot,
         rankdir = TB]

  node [shape = rectangle,
        fontname = Helvetica,
        fontsize = 10,
        width = 2.8,
        style = filled,
        fillcolor = White,
        color = Black]

  # Nodes
  n0  [label = 'lightning-Caused Wildfires in Alberta (1996-2002)']
  n1  [label = 'All Recorded Wildfires in Database \\n(n ≈ 960)']
  n2  [label = 'Fires where IA occurred between the hours of 23h00 and 04h00\\n(n = 6)']
  n3  [label = 'Fires with Data Coding Errors\\n(Negative Growth)\\n(n = 65)']
  n4  [label = 'Retained for Analysis \\n(n=889)']
  n5  [label = 'Included in Survival Analysis\\n(n = 260)']
  n6  [label = 'No growth \\n(Excluded from Survival Analysis)\\n(n = 629)']

  # Ranks for nicer vertical alignment
  {rank = same; n2}
  {rank = same; n5 n6}

  # Edges
  edge [fontname = Helvetica,
        fontsize = 9]

  n0 -> n1
  n1 -> n2 [label = 'Excluded']
  n1 -> n3 [label = 'Excluded']
  n1 -> n4 [label = 'Included in Analysis']
  n4 -> n5 [label = 'Included in Survival Analysis']
  n4 -> n6 [label = 'Excluded from Survival Analysis']

}
")


```
